calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement And Enter Order For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-25-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Hengameh Kishani, Esq. of Temmerman Cilley & Kohlmann LLP, Tel.408.998.9500, for Petitioner Frank Leeds;
Jeremiah Armstrong, Esq. of The Law Office of Craig S. Miller, Tel. 415.296.7070, appearing for Respondents David Leeds & Victoria Leeds.

Hearing on Petitioner Frank Leeds' Motion to Enforce the Settlement Agreement and Enter Order for Sanctions.

Matter called, argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows: Petitioner Frank Leeds' Motion to Enforce the Settlement Agreement is GRANTED. Objectors David Leeds and Victoria Leeds have failed to comply with completing the 1031 Exchange, including failing to execute any and all documents reasonably necessary to complete the exchange, in violation of the Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release, dated August 28, 2019, and the First Amendment to Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release, dated December 22, 2020. Victoria and David Leeds shall take all steps necessary to execute the 1031 exchange documents in accordance with the settlement agreement, as amended, within 30 days of entry of this Order.

Petitioner's request for attorney fees pursuant to the settlement agreements is GRANTED in the amount of $2,769.

Ms. Kishani is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. The Court notes that Mr. Armstrong is currently on vacation and may not be able to provide a signature at this time. The proposed order and any contested portions are to be submitted to the Court via the clerk's email which was provided in open court.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter not reported. Bailiff: not present.

Off Calendar May-17-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER FRANK LEEDS' Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement, Enter Judgment, And For Attorneys Fees [CCP Sec. 128,664.6].

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. Motion needs to be filed in dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Trustees David H. Leeds And Victoria E. Leeds Petition For Instructions Requesting Trustees Authority To Use Trust Funds To Defend Against Contest
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Cilley appeared by zoom for Frank Leeds.
Michael Wanser appeared by zoom for David Leeds and Victoria Leeds.

Mr. Cilley stated that they implemented the terms of the settlement which required an exchange of deeds, that there is a 1031 exchange issue, that they are in touch with the CPA and that it has taken longer than they anticipated. Mr. Cilley also stated that the parties feel they are far along enough to file a dismissal. The Court stated that it does not know why a dismissal is needed, that the settlement was approved by the Court and ordered that the petitions go off calendar. The Petition for Instructions Requesting Trustee's Authority to Use Trust Funds to Defend Against Contest, and the Petition to Invalidate Exercises of Powers of Appointment and Letter of Instruction are off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Trustees David H. Leeds And Victoria E. Leeds Petition For Instructions Requesting Trustees Authority To Use Trust Funds To Defend Against Contest
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Continued To Sep-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Cilley appeared by Zoom for Frank Leeds.

Mr. Cilley stated that they entered into a settlement agreement that envisions trading of two properties, that they are trying to transfer the properties via 1031 exchange and that they are in contact with the Accountants to achieve a tax advantage. Mr. Cilley also stated that he had recent communication with Mr. Greenberg's Associate, that they are trying to bring the Accountants together and that he is not sure if they knew about today's hearing. Mr. Cilley also stated that Mr. Wasner was present at the last hearing, that he believes they were e-mailed by the Court of the continuance and that he does not believe they need the settlement agreement to be approved by the Court. The Petition to Invalidate Exercises of Powers of Appointment, etc., and the Petition for Instructions, etc. are continued to September 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the dismissal. If the dismissal is filed by September 8, 2020, no appearance will be required. Ms. Cilley is to give notice to the other parties of the hearing date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Wanser for Objector, David Leeds and Victoria Leeds via Court Call.
Erin Norcia for Petitioner, Frank Leeds via Court Call.

The Court noted that the petition to approve settlement agreement would resolve the underlying petitions, noting that there are no objections it is inclined to grant this petition subject to review of written order and inquired if the other petitions can be taken off calendar. Ms. Norcia stated that she will need to ask the counsel she is appearing for to confirm. Mr. Wanser stated that the settlement agreement stated the petitions are to be dismissed with prejudice within 28 days. Ms. Norcia stated she will comply with the terms of the settlement agreement and file a dismissal.

The Court granted the petition to approve settlement agreement (subject to review of the order).

The Petition to invalidate exercises of powers for appointment and letters of instruction, and Trustees David H. Leeds and Victoria E. Leeds petition for instructions requesting trustees authority to use trust funds to defend against contest are continued to March 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Exercises Of Powers Of Appointment And Letter Of Instruction
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Continued To Oct-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Schmuck for Frank Leeds.
Michael Wanser for David Leeds and Victoria Leeds (Respondents/Petitioners).

Mr. Schmuck stated that they participated in mediation on August 28th before Judge Gallagher, that they had to cut it short because he received a call regarding a family matter and that they continued to negotiate in the last week. Mr. Schmuck also requested continuing the petition for instructions out, setting the contest for trial (7 days) and stated that there will be some discovery. The Court informed Counsel that it will not set the contest for trial until it is ready. The Petition to Invalidate Exercises of Powers of Appointment, etc. and the Petition for Instructions Requesting Trustees Authority to Use Trust Funds to Defend Against Contest are continued to October 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Wanser's reply is due by October 22, 2019. If the matter is not settled on October 29th, the parties will present argument on the petition for instructions and the Court will make a decision based on what is presented.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Invalidate Exercises Of Powers Of Appointment And Letter Of Instruction
Off Calendar Mar-26-2019 Continued To Jun-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Schmuck for and with Frank Leeds (Petitioner).
Michael Wanser appearing by CourtCall for David Leeds & Victoria Leeds (Respondents).

Mr. Schmuck stated that they just received the responses yesterday, that they need to propound some written discovery, that they'd like time to review the discovery responses, and that he was requesting that the Court allow time for that to happen. Mr. Wanser concurred. The Court continued the matter to June 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a referral to mediation or trial setting. The Court inquired about whether or not the matter would be eligible for pro bono mediation. Mr. Schmuck stated that he did not know and deferred to Mr. Wanser. Mr. Wanser stated that he did think that the matter would be eligible for pro bono mediation.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.
